kept.text:
$PLTR & National Health Serivce UK Deployment

The National Health Service (NHS) in England has long grappled with one of the most formidable operational challenges in modern healthcare: fragmented data systems. Serving approximately 57 million patients with 1.5 million staff across hundreds of hospital trusts, the NHS has historically relied on decades-old legacy infrastructure where critical information patient records, waiting lists, operating theatre schedules, and discharge plans existed in silos. A recent promotional article from Palantir Technologies, published on LinkedIn on April 30, 2026, titled “Ready, Set, Build with the NHS Federated Data Platform,” presents the NHS Federated Data Platform (FDP) as a bold solution to this fragmentation. Delivered through a consortium led by Palantir on its Foundry platform, the FDP aims to connect data safely, enable actionable insights, and foster innovation. While the article highlights measurable impacts and grassroots development, a fuller analysis reveals both genuine potential and significant hurdles in adoption, ethics, and long-term sustainability.

At its core, the FDP employs a federated architecture that respects local control while enabling system-wide interoperability. Each NHS organization operates its own “tenant,” maintaining data controller status, with information remaining under local governance. A shared Canonical Data Model standardizes representations of data, logic, actions, and security policies, allowing nationally developed tools (for waiting list validation, discharge coordination, and theatre scheduling) to function alongside locally built applications. Privacy safeguards including encryption, purpose-based access, and Privacy Enhancing Technologies for de-identification are emphasized as foundational. This design theoretically balances the need for integration with data protection requirements, addressing decades of failed centralization attempts in the NHS.

Trusts using the platform reportedly enabled 110,078 additional theatre procedures, safely removed 797,728 patients from waiting lists, facilitated 297,943 discharges with around a 15% reduction in delays, and improved 28-day cancer referral responses by 6.8%. These figures, if sustained and verified, represent meaningful relief for patients and staff amid chronic NHS pressures. Beyond core products, the platform’s extensibility was showcased in the inaugural “Build with FDP” 48-hour hackathon, where over 120 NHS developers, analysts, clinicians, and operational staff created 25 prototypes. Examples include an early warning system for patient deterioration, bed demand prediction tools, CAMHS workflow balancers, and genomic-optimized waitlist recommendations. Such events underscore a vision of clinician-led innovation scaling nationally through a common infrastructure. 

Yet the FDP’s rollout has not been seamless. Independent reporting indicates slower-than-expected adoption, with fewer than a quarter of England’s roughly 215 hospital trusts actively using the platform by late 2024, rising to around one-third by mid-2025 in some accounts. Implementation costs for individual trusts—requiring additional developers, project managers, and integration work—have drawn criticism, as have assessments from some NHS insiders that the software itself is “not very good.” These practical challenges highlight the gap between promotional narratives and on-the-ground realities in a vast, decentralized system.

More contentious are the ethical and political dimensions surrounding Palantir’s central role. The U.S. firm’s background in defense, intelligence, and controversial applications (such as immigration enforcement) has fueled opposition from the British Medical Association, unions, human rights organizations, and patient groups. Critics argue that awarding a £330 million-plus contract (with total costs potentially higher) to a foreign company with intelligence ties risks patient trust, data sovereignty, and long-term vendor lock-in. Palantir maintains strict contractual limits—no commercialization of NHS data, no use for training external models—and NHS England stresses that intellectual property arrangements protect public interests. Nevertheless, calls persist for reduced engagement or an eventual exit, reflecting deeper unease about outsourcing sensitive health infrastructure.

The FDP exemplifies broader tensions in public sector digital transformation. On one hand, leveraging commercial platforms like Foundry offers speed and sophistication that in-house development might struggle to match, especially given historical NHS IT shortcomings. The ontology-driven approach and federated model represent thoughtful engineering for a complex national system. On the other, heavy reliance on a single vendor raises questions of dependency, cost-effectiveness, and public accountability. The hackathon’s emphasis on local problem-solving is encouraging, but its success will ultimately depend on whether prototypes translate into scalable, maintainable tools without entrenching external control.

Palantir paints an optimistic picture of the NHS FDP as a platform that not only connects data but empowers action and innovation. Early metrics suggest real benefits for patients through reduced waits and better operational flow. However, modest adoption rates, implementation frictions, and intense ethical debates indicate that the platform’s full potential remains contested. For the NHS a cherished institution serving an entire nation the FDP’s ultimate value will be measured not by promotional claims or hackathon prototypes, but by sustained improvements in care delivery, robust safeguards for patient data, and the ability to evolve independently over time. As the next “Build with FDP” events unfold, the initiative stands as both a test of technological ambition and a mirror to the UK’s ongoing struggle to modernize its health service without compromising its founding principles.

Source: https://t.co/qfaOE6y8hb

kept.text:
$PLTR Palantir: Data Deciphering Giant 👊🏻💥
TIME100 Most Influential Companies of 2026
Today, TIME reveals the sixth annual TIME100 Most Influential Companies list, highlighting 100 companies making extraordinary impact around the world.

Apr 30, 2026
Palantir has something for everyone. The 23-year-old company makes software platforms—into which it embeds AI—that help organizations understand and make decisions with their data. Its more than 950 clients include the U.S. Department of Defense, the U.K. National Health Service, and Airbus. Across finance, logistics, manufacturing, energy, telecommunications, and more, the value proposition is the same: let our engineers plug your data into our platforms, so you can do your work better.

Some of this work has generated controversy; In May 2025, Palantir was awarded a $795 million modification to its contract with the U.S. Army to license its Maven Smart System—the same system which, since February, has reportedly been used in the war in Iran to identify strike targets. In July, the Army consolidated 75 contracts into a new agreement allowing it and other Defense Department agencies to buy up to $10 billion of Palantir software over a 10-year period. And for years, the company has been criticized for its work building systems for U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). But the company’s commercial business is now roughly as large as its government work, and growing fast. In February, Palantir announced its most profitable quarter to date, generating $1.4 billion in revenue—a 70% increase over a year prior.

To the critics, the company says it’s not responsible for how others use its tech. “Palantir doesn’t impose a normative view on our customers,” says Ryan Taylor, the company’s chief revenue and chief legal officer. “We're the data experts. We're the platform experts. Our customers are the experts in their space—we help our customers operate in their space more effectively.”
by: Tharin Pillay
👏🏻🦾🏆🔮🦄🔥
https://t.co/H1Sz9LO3lS

$PLTR Oppenheimer Initiates Palantir Stock At Outperform With Q1 Earnings Due 🦾🔥

04/30/2026
Oppenheimer on Thursday initiated coverage of Palantir Technologies (PLTR) with an outperform rating ahead of the software maker's first quarter earnings report due Monday. Palantir stock edged up amid the news.

Oppenheimer analyst Param Singh in a report gave Palantir stock a price target of 200. That's roughly 40% above where shares in the maker of data analytics software are now trading.

"While Palantir's high valuation remains a concern for the investment community, we see it as justified as Palantir is rapidly becoming the leading platform for AI application deployment across the government and commercial sectors," Singh said in a report.

He added that Palantir's "custom applications based on an ontology (digital-mapping)-anchored system have considerable switching costs once embedded, creating a highly sticky platform."

"Palantir is rapidly expanding within the traditional commercial enterprise organizations and establishing itself as the "best-in-class" vendor for this market segment," Singh said...
🐂📈🦾🦄🔥

kept.text:
$PLTR Partner LG CNS Reports Strong Q1 Revenue and Profit: "In particular, we are strengthening our AX (AI transformation) business by offering various services where multi-agents based on agentic AI collaborate autonomously." BizChosun also reports that LG CNS has supplied ChatGPT Enterprise since February and secured about ten corporations as customers, and that cooperation with Palantir includes forward deployed engineer teams to identify AX projects. 🦾🔮💥

April 30, 2026
Reporting by BizChosun states that LG CNS posted first-quarter revenue of 1.315 trillion won, up 8.6% year-on-year, and operating profit of 94.2 billion won, up 19.4%, driven by growth in its AI and cloud businesses. According to BizChosun, revenue from AI and cloud rose 6.7% to 765.4 billion won, roughly 58% of total revenue. 

BizChosun includes direct quotes from an LG CNS official: "In AI, we have diversified our portfolio across all industries..." and "In particular, we are strengthening our AX (AI transformation) business by offering various services where multi-agents based on agentic AI collaborate autonomously." The report also cites recent deals including ChatGPT Enterprise deployments and collaboration with Palantir, and large data-center projects. 🦾🔮

According to BizChosun, LG CNS reported first-quarter revenue of 1.315 trillion won, a year-on-year increase of 8.6%, and operating profit of 94.2 billion won, up 19.4%. Reporting by BizChosun states that revenue from AI and cloud rose 6.7% year-on-year to 765.4 billion won, representing about 58% of total revenue. 

BizChosun quotes an LG CNS official saying, "In AI, we have diversified our portfolio across all industries, including public, defense, finance, manufacturing, pharmaceuticals and bio, shipbuilding, and defense industry," and, "In particular, we are strengthening our AX (AI transformation) business by offering various services where multi-agents based on agentic AI collaborate autonomously." 🦾

BizChosun also reports that LG CNS has supplied ChatGPT Enterprise since February and secured about ten corporations as customers, and that cooperation with Palantir includes forward deployed engineer teams to identify AX projects. The report further notes cloud-related wins, including projects worth over 1 trillion won at the Samsong data center and a modular AI data center offering. 🔥

Editorial analysis - technical context
Companies deploying enterprise AI transformation (AX) typically combine cloud partnerships, enterprise LLM access, and systems-integration services. For system integrators, revenue shifts toward recurring software and managed services and higher-margin cloud design-build-operate (DBO) contracts commonly increase gross-profit contribution. Multi-agent and agentic architectures cited in the reporting align with a broader industry move to orchestration layers that coordinate specialized models and tool use rather than monolithic large-model deployments.

Industry context
Observed patterns in similar transitions show that tie-ups with hyperscalers and specialized vendors like Palantir often serve two roles: accelerating technical delivery on data and orchestration, and providing commercial channels to large enterprise customers. For practitioners, the mix of ChatGPT Enterprise deployments plus DBO data-center projects underscores the ongoing coupling of model access, data infrastructure, and integration work in enterprise AI rollouts.

What to watch
Indicators an observer can track include reported growth in recurring AI/cloud revenue as a share of total revenue, the pipeline of AX projects converting to long-term managed-services contracts, and announced customer wins for ChatGPT Enterprise or similar enterprise LLM products. Market signals from partners such as Palantir and hyperscalers about joint deployments will also show whether integration efforts are scaling beyond pilot phases...
🇰🇷🦾🔮🔥

kept.text:
$PLTR Palantir (PLTR) Will Report Q1 Earnings on May 4. Wall Street Analysts Eye 115% Earnings Jump

May 01, 2026
Palantir Technologies will announce its results for the first quarter of 2026 on Monday, May 4. PLTR stock surged about 20% in 2025, driven by fast growth across its Commercial business and wider use of its Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP). 

Wall Street expects Palantir to report EPS (earnings per share) of $0.28 for Q1 2026, reflecting 115% year-over-year growth. Revenue is projected to rise 74% to $1.54 billion.

Investors will be watching for updates on commercial customer growth, AIP adoption, government contract momentum, and management’s forward guidance when the company reports its earnings...

Ahead of the print, Top Baird analyst William Power reaffirmed an Outperform rating and a $200 price target, stating that he expects “another strong quarter” from the software giant. He said revenue growth could accelerate for the 11th straight quarter, driven by strength in both U.S. Commercial and Government segments.

Beyond this quarter, Power expects growth to continue into 2026 and 2027, with free cash flow potentially reaching $7.5 billion by 2027. He also sees the recent pause in the stock as a good entry point for investors looking to buy into a high-growth name...
By: Shalu Saraf
🍿📈🦾🔮💥

kept.text:
$PLTR X Northern Re Northern Re to scale capital intelligence via Palantir platform selection 👀🦾

Northern Re, the collateralized reinsurance company that provides investors access to returns of its long-tail casualty underwriting business, has announced that it has selected Palantir Technologies as a core platform to support the continued development of its capital intelligence capabilities.

According to the announcement, the platform brings underwriting and capital teams onto a shared view of portfolio performance, which helps support clearer decision‑making as programs develop over time.

Northern Re’s decision to choose Palantir reportedly builds on work that the company has been doing for a number of years to improve how operational data is used on a day-to-day basis, rather than solely relying on multiple disconnected systems.

Palantir is a globally recognised data integration, analytics, and artificial intelligence-driven software specialist.

Northern Re uses one platform to track how risk develops across the life of a transaction. However, the company confirmed that Palantir will be able to further support this function by helping its team bring information together and work with it consistently, from initial underwriting through to ongoing portfolio oversight.

Additionally, Northern Re noted that the platform is intended to strengthen governance, improve visibility of portfolio performance, as well as support consistent decision‑making as the business continues to grow. 💥

The selection of Palantir follows a period of growth for Northern Re, during which the company has expanded across multiple jurisdictions and increased the scale and complexity of the transactions it supports.

Earlier this year, we reported that Northern Re had raised an additional $150 million in committed capital from existing and new institutional investors, bringing the company’s total platform capacity to $325 million.

Peter McKelvy, Co‑Founder of Northern Re, commented: “Coming from a product development background, velocity and time-to-value are always top of mind. Development on Palantir has moved even faster than expected. We’ve been able to build quickly and see real impact early on. 🔥

“We also share a clear view on keeping humans closely involved in critical decisions. We embrace AI, but accuracy matters too much for blind automation, and the platform supports that balance.” 🎯

Anthony McKelvy, Co‑Founder of Northern Re, said: “Each time we engage with a cedent, we work to understand what they are trying to achieve, whether that is capital relief, earnings stability or something more structural.

“Looking at those factors in isolation only gets you so far. What matters is being able to assess them together, clearly and quickly. That is what Northern Re does well and exactly what we are continuing to build on Palantir.” 🦄
🦾🚀🔮🦄💥
https://t.co/cezWMRJDZE

tweet.text:
📢 𝐉𝐔𝐒𝐓 𝐈𝐍: Nvidia, Microsoft, AWS Expanding Classified Military AI Use - $NVDA $MSFT $AMZN $PLTR 

The Pentagon has signed agreements with Nvidia, Microsoft, Reflection AI and Amazon Web Services to deploy advanced AI tools on classified networks, expanding a roster that includes SpaceX, OpenAI and Google. The Defense Department said the deals enable lawful operational use and advance its goal of becoming an “AI-first” military. 

Officials confirmed the arrangements anonymously. AWS said it will continue supporting military modernization, while Microsoft declined comment and Nvidia and Reflection did not respond.

이 두기업은 실적 좋을 것 같습니다.

대형 기업들 대부분 실적 발표했고 다음주 중소형주 실적 발표 기다리고 있습니다. 그동안 어떤 뉴스로 휩쓸렸던 부분에 대해서 만회해주지 않을까 싶습니다.
근데 그것과 장기적인 전망은 좀 다른 부분이 있습니다.
저도 소프트웨어 기업 좋게 보지만 이번에 실적 보고 어느 정도 매도하고 갈아타기 준비중입니다.

quoted_tweet.text:
실적 시즌 집중하세요!
4월 30일 미국시장전 브리핑

오늘 4월 마지막 거래일입니다.
오늘 엄청나게 많은 회사들의 실적이 나왔습니다.
우리는 주식 투자를 하는 사람이고 주식 투자의 메커니즘을 이해해야 합니다. 이런 실적과 가이던스는 주가가 오르는 것인지 내리는 것인지를 판단 구분하는 것이 가장 핵심입니다.
믿음만으로 비이성적으로 투자하는 사람의 수익률이 높긴 힘듭니다.

$GOOGL 실적도 좋고 가이던스도 좋습니다. 오를 수 밖에 없습니다.
$META 실적 좋았으나 Capex 투자가 크게 증가하고 회사채 발행 소식도 떠서 주가 안 좋습니다.
$AMZN 실적 좋았고 가이던스가 좋았습니다.
$MSFT 실적 좋았는데 다음 분기 가이던스가 약간 미스했고 Capex 조금 증가했습니다.
$LLY 실적 좋았고 가이던스도 좀 더 상향했습니다.

제가 하는 트레이딩 기법중에 기술분석에 기반하여 실적 때 하는 트레이딩도 있습니다. 하루에 3~10%도 먹을 수 있습니다.
무조건 실적 발표를 보고 주가가 오를지 내릴지&현재 주가 위치를 같이 보면 쉽게 할 수 있습니다.
여러분도 한번 해보시길 추천드립니다.

#GOOGL #구글 
#META #메타
#AMZN #아마존
#MSFT #마이크로소프트
#LLY #일라이릴리
